title: zanthoxylum punctatum subsp. obtriangulare
intro: Zanthoxylum punctatum subsp. obtriangulare, also known as the yellow pepper tree, is a plant species within the rutaceae family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and unique triangular leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum punctatum subsp. obtriangulare include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as potential antimicrobial effects that can aid in wound healing and immune system support. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying and processing the leaves and fruits to create teas, infusions, or topical applications for various health benefits.
